content: In this lecture, Ian Ball explains subgame-perfect Nash equilibriums, a strategy profile in which every player’s strategy is optimal not just for the game as a whole, but at every possible decision point (subgame) within the game.
title: Lecture 10: Subgame-Perfect Nash Equilibrium

video_metadata.video_tags: 14-12-economic-applications-of-game-theory-fall-2025, backward induction, credibility, nash equilbrium, optimal, sequential rationality, subgame
video_metadata.youtube_description:
MIT 14.12 Economic Applications of Game Theory, Fall 2025 
Instructor: Ian Ball 
View the complete course: https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/14-12-economic-applications-of-game-theory-fall-2025/
YouTube Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLUl4u3cNGP63quuKvMHCt3cmTmt0O2qpv

In this lecture, Ian Ball explains subgame-perfect Nash equilibriums, which is a strategy profile where every player's strategy is optimal not just for the game as a whole, but at every possible decision point (subgame) within the game.
